description: At least one reasoning backend is reachable through a policy-driven adapter: codex-cli, agy-cli, anthropic, openrouter, nemotron-api, local, Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, llama.cpp, MLX, LocalAI, or any future adapter registered in the reasoning route policy. The legacy gemini-cli adapter remains available for explicit Enterprise configurations. Stub backend is always available; this manifest distinguishes 'real backend present' from 'falling back to stub'.

capabilities.description: Any of the supported real backends is reachable. Without one, Kyberion runs in stub mode (deterministic placeholders, no LLM output).

install.instruction: Pick one and authenticate it. Recommended for personal use: configure `codex` or `agy` (Antigravity CLI). Other supported choices are OpenRouter (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY` or `KYBERION_OPENROUTER_KEY`), `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, or an OpenAI-compatible endpoint such as Nemotron, Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, llama.cpp, MLX, or LocalAI. Then set `KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND` to the corresponding id (`codex-cli` / `agy-cli` / `anthropic` / `openrouter` / `nemotron-api` / `local` / `ollama` / `vllm` / `lmstudio` / `llamacpp` / `mlx` / `localai`; use `gemini-cli` only when intentionally configured). Setting it to `stub` is also valid for offline / deterministic-test deployments.

capabilities.description: KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND is set so the bootstrap chooses deterministically rather than auto-discovering on every start.

install.instruction: Set `KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND` to one of `codex-cli` / `gemini-cli` / `agy-cli` / `anthropic` / `claude-agent` / `openrouter` / `nemotron-api` / `local` / `ollama` / `vllm` / `lmstudio` / `llamacpp` / `mlx` / `localai` / `stub`. Without it, the bootstrap auto-discovers from registered adapters and may pick a different backend across runs. Use `pnpm reasoning:config` to bind roles without changing the global backend; use `pnpm reasoning:config doctor` or Operator Surface `/reasoning` to inspect effective fallback readiness.
